title: The importance of land acknowledgments
summary: A land acknowledgment is a formal way to recognize the Indigenous peoples who have come before you, and who were removed — often violently — from their land due to colonization. Cover: Interactive map via Native Land Digital . By: Kayla Heidenreich I, Kayla Heidenreich, have written this on the traditional lands and territories of the Indigenous Nooksack and Coast Salish Tribes. Alaska Wilderness League recognizes that our offices are located on the traditional territories of the Dena’ina (Anchorage), Anacostan (D.C....
